name: Dianthus Therapeutics, Inc. /DE/
business: Dianthus Therapeutics is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing therapies for severe autoimmune diseases. Its lead candidate, claseprubart, is a monoclonal antibody targeting active C1s with extended half-life and subcutaneous self-administration. Its second candidate, DNTH212, is a bifunctional fusion protein targeting BDCA2 and BAFF/APRIL.
sellsTo: Patients with Chronic Inflammatory Demyelinating Polyneuropathy (CIDP)
Patients with other autoimmune diseases where classical pathway is implicated

exposures.sentence: We are a clinical-stage biotechnology company dedicated to developing potentially best-in-class therapies for patients living with severe autoimmune diseases.

Our lead clinical-stage candidate, claseprubart, is a monoclonal antibody that is purposefully engineered with extended half-life, improved potency, and high selectivity for only the active C1s complement protein.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 3 registrational trial in gMG
catalysts.sentence: We expect to initiate a Phase 3 registrational trial in gMG in mid-2026 and report top-line results in the second half of 2028.

catalysts.event: Report top-line results from Phase 3 gMG trial
catalysts.sentence: We expect to initiate a Phase 3 registrational trial in gMG in mid-2026 and report top-line results in the second half of 2028.

catalysts.event: Top-line results from Phase 2 MMN trial
catalysts.sentence: We anticipate initial top-line results from this trial will be available in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Top-line results from Phase 1 healthy volunteers for DNTH212
catalysts.sentence: A two-part Phase 1 study in China in healthy volunteers (Part A) and patients with systemic lupus erythematosus (Part B) was initiated in December 2025, with top-line results in healthy volunteers expected in the second half of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: More than 100,000 individuals in the United States with AChR+ gMG
tamClaims.sentence: Based on Komodo claims data, we believe there are more than 100,000 individuals in the United States with acetylcholine receptor antibody-positive gMG.

tamClaims.claim: CIDP estimated to affect more than 40,000 people in the United States
tamClaims.sentence: CIDP is estimated to affect more than 40,000 people in the United States.
It is an underrecognized disease with U.S. prevalence estimates of approximately 10,000 individuals.
